Computing walk-forward fragility & directed-spillover…

RESILIENCE TELEMETRY

cross-asset fragility & directed-spillover console · descriptive, not predictive
Regime: —
—
Why this console borrows the St. Pete water dashboard's lenses but not its foresight ▾
The water console can warn hours ahead because the physical system gives it four things a market does not: (1) fixed surveyed topology — the pipe graph and elevations are ground truth that never has to be guessed and never rewires; (2) conservation — water piles up in a tank of known size and travels pipes at a known speed, so level + rate-of-rise gives a calculable time-to-overflow; (3) exogenous drivers with real lead time — rain, tide, and surge are coming whether or not anyone forecasts them, and don't change course because operators reacted; (4) hidden-state telemetry — SCADA reads a level only the operator can see. Markets invert every one: the cross-asset network here is estimated from noisy returns and rewires the instant a crisis hits (correlations collapse to one mode); nothing is conserved — a crowded position unwinds in milliseconds through price, so there is no quantity to integrate forward to a guaranteed overflow; the would-be drivers (Fed, CPI, flows) are endogenous and already priced, leaving only the unforecastable surprise; information moves at ~0 transport delay and is arbitraged across venues, so any stable upstream-leads-downstream edge gets traded to lag 0; and price is the expectation, so there's no hidden state to telemeter (and watching the gauge perturbs it — reflexivity). So the new panels (spectral radius, absorption ratio, turbulence, Hawkes, timed lead-lag) are built as a systemic-stress thermometer: by their own authors' admission these rise during 2008/2020/2022, not before. The only weakly-leading construct is the standardized absorption-ratio shift, and even that is sample-dependent and hasn't held out-of-sample. Net: the water dashboard's predictive factors do not confer market-timing skill — which is exactly why everything here stays descriptive, not predictive.

Behavioral guardrail — brake yourself, not the market strong evidence · about you

The one feature here that measurably improves outcomes — and it's about your behaviour, not the market. It audits your decisions, holds your pre-commitments, and adds a pause exactly where panic happens. Walled off from every market reading by design.

Volatility-sizing dial — a down-only throttle risk, not alpha

Size down when forecast volatility exceeds the comfort band you type — it caps at 100% (never levers up); you set the band and pull the lever. Shown with the honest drawdown↔turnover trade-off, no Sharpe.

Crash-context badge — the regime you're already in coincident, not a forecast

A pure annotation: turbulence, one-factor absorption, shock clustering and skew read together as the current tail regime. Wired to nothing — it moves no dial and predicts nothing.

Diversification reality check fair-weather caveat

How many bets you really have and how few names carry your risk — with the honest warning that it's computed on calm correlations that collapse in a crisis.

Contrarian sentiment thermometer context only · gutted

Panic / neutral / euphoria from VIX, valuation and turbulence extremes — shown with the forward-return distribution from here as proof it's a thermometer, not a clock. No trade, no buy/sell, ever.

Scenario stress-test — synthetic crises to size against FHS · not a forecast

The transparent cousin of a generative model: thousands of synthetic next-month paths, each resampling real vol-standardized moves re-scaled to today's volatility. A fuller range of bad months than the few real crises — to stress-test your sizing, never to predict.

Drift & rebalance — a state, not an alert band, not a trigger

Where a year of relative performance has pushed your mix off the target weights you set in the Crisis-replay card — shown as a passive state. Never a red action; rebalance on a rule, not a signal.

Stock-bond ballast read describes the past quarter

Whether bonds are currently diversifying your equities or moving with them (like 2022) — present-tense context for your ballast, with no persistence claim and no allocation steer.

Directed spillover network — who leads whom

Lagged cross-correlation asymmetry (LCCA): an arrow i→j means i's past returns forecast j's future more than the reverse, over the analysis window. Permutation-gated (only links beyond a circular-shift null are drawn). Red = net transmitter, blue = net receiver. Lead-lag is predictive association, not causation. Global note: the international ETFs (pink) trade US hours, so any "lead" the US shows over them is partly mechanical — they reprice overnight foreign news during the US session — not pure information flow.
net transmitter (leads) net receiver (lags)

Asymmetry matrix & net transmitter / receiver

Left: directed asymmetry A(i→j)=D(i→j)−D(j→i), anti-symmetric (red i leads j, blue j leads i, near-white = pure co-movement, no leader). Right: net directional spillover per asset (right/red transmits, left/blue receives).

Cascade simulation — shock propagation descriptive drill

Inject a shock at one market and propagate it along the directed spillover network — the way a failure cascades through a water system. Linear-threshold model on the gated lead-lag edges. A what-if on the current estimated network, not a forecast.

Network structure — hubs, choke points & amplification graph-theoretic

Borrowing from evolutionary graph theory and metabolic pathways: where the spillover network concentrates (preferential-attachment hubs), which market is its structural bottleneck (choke point), and whether its topology amplifies or damps a shock.

Systemic coupling — spectral radius ρ̂ & absorption ratio

How tightly the whole basket is moving as one thing. ρ̂=λ_max/N is the dominant eigenvalue of the rolling cross-asset correlation matrix (your LSAxJEPA spectral radius ρ(Φ) / κ_B coupling proxy); the absorption ratio is the variance share in the top modes (Kritzman-Li). High = little local shock-absorption capacity. This is the closest honest analog of the water system's fixed topology — except here the topology is estimated and rewires in a crisis.

Factor baskets — what actually moves together hierarchical · empirical

Your nominal nine asset classes are highly redundant. This clusters the basket by its real correlation structure into the handful of independent factors that actually drive it — the "actual baskets," not the labels.

Game-theoretic lens — beauty contest & coordination risk framing

The strategic structure behind the numbers: why direction cannot be forecast (higher-order beliefs), and when the system is vulnerable to a coordinated run (global games).

Resting bid vs ask size — live book microstructure crypto · seconds · descriptive

A live microstructure readout — not a rung on a prediction ladder: how much resting size currently sits on the bid vs the ask of the Binance.US book (top 20 levels), refreshing every few seconds. Descriptive flow, not a forecast: any move it anticipates is smaller than the spread and decays in seconds, so it is not directional alpha and not tradable through a browser. A skewed book is liquidity resting there now — not a buy or sell call. Crypto only (the only free live order book); live mode only.

Financial turbulence — system-level surprise (Mahalanobis)

How statistically unusual today's whole cross-asset move is vs its recent covariance (Kritzman-Li). A multivariate extension of the per-asset surprise / variational-free-energy lens.
0.20

Structural geography — market-state manifold (diffusion map)

The nonlinear generalization of the coupling panel: every dot is a 10-day cross-asset state of the macro names, placed by a diffusion map so similar regimes sit together. Calm states cluster; crises (2020/2022) form a stressed arm. Where today sits — and which past state it most resembles — is a coincident read on the regime, never a forecast. Realizes the geometric side of your LSAxJEPA framework (nonlinear generalization of PCA/spectral structure).

Anticipation — where to act, & does the transmitter actually lead? predictive test

The companion paper's actual engine made live: a VAR + Diebold–Yılmaz directed volatility-spillover decomposition picks the net transmitter (who exports stress) vs the loudest (most-volatile) market, and runs an out-of-sample test of whether the transmitter truly leads. Maps where anticipation has structural leverage — not a price forecast.
Alpha Lab — walk-forward stress→trade backtest research · net-of-cost
A backtest, not sold alpha. It tests, honestly, whether the stress signal monetizes: net-of-cost PnL and an SPY buy&hold benchmark lead; gross is buried support. Direction is a chosen stance — the data gives you stress, not direction. The likely honest result is the edge bleeds out after friction.
Crisis replay — your mix through past stress windows historical · not predictive
Set the weights you choose and replay them, daily-rebalanced, through a real past stress window. Pure historical replay of data already in the console — no model, no forecast, no recommendation. It shows how a mix actually held up, and how its protection shrank as assets coupled.
Premia allocator + stress-test — your passive-income plan, made concrete education · not advice
Set how much you put in each risk premium and see the honest tradeoff: the income you'd passively collect, an illustrative total return, and the worst-case drawdown your mix actually took through 2020 — the price of that income. This is the plan's core lesson in one place: higher income always buys a deeper drawdown. No forecast, no recommendation.

Forward-volatility lab — predict what's predictable purged CV

The constructive flip side of the Alpha Lab: a ridge model on the risk-state features, evaluated with purged + embargoed cross-validation, predicting next-5- and 20-day realized volatility (learnable) versus direction (flat at both horizons). Shows where the data actually carries information.

Forecast reliability — the meta-learning layer regime-conditional

A layer above the forecasts: it does not predict — it says when each forecast can be trusted. Volatility is learnable, but more so in some regimes than others; direction is learnable in none. Use it to gate and size, not to call direction.

Overnight vs intraday — the one free directional crumb risk premium · show, not trade

Asked "what free data predicts short-term direction?", the honest answer is: almost nothing — except this. Splitting each day into its overnight (close→open) and intraday (open→close) halves reveals a real, documented, index-level directional regularity. It is a liquidity-provision risk premium, not tradable alpha — shown as the closest free data gets, and why it still is not money.

Long-horizon valuation thermometer years · descriptive

What the canon — Cochrane's discount-rate revolution and Shiller's CAPE — identifies as the one genuinely directional free-data signal, and it lives only at the multi-year horizon, with wide bands and a documented out-of-sample-failure record. Where we sit versus 150 years of history.

Factor companion — harvested premia (1–12 month) cross-asset · descriptive

The cross-asset / time-series premia the canon (Ilmanen, AQR) identifies as genuinely moving relative returns at the 1–12 month horizon — trend, carry, low-risk — read off prices the console already holds. Harvested statically, never timed.

Cross-sectional factor demo — what "doing what quants do" returns honesty exhibit · no edge

The genuinely accessible version of the pros' playbook: switch the target to cross-sectional (rank 85 stocks), manufacture breadth, combine three documented free-data factors (12−1 momentum · 1-month reversal · low-vol) in a no-fit composite, market-neutral quintile long-short, monthly. Frozen result of a real backtest — and a demonstration of why it returns nothing.
⚠ Survivorship: universe = 85 current (2026-picked) large-caps — failed/delisted names excluded. This inflates momentum/reversal and may flip low-vol's sign; the true effect is ≤ what is shown. Picking liquid mega-caps separately deflates the real premia (which live in small/illiquid names). The number is squeezed from both sides.
Is the signal there?
Composite rank-IC (forward 1mo, out-of-sample)+0.021
95% CI · p-value[−0.021, +0.062] · p=0.33 — indistinguishable from zero
t-stat vs the field's discovery hurdlet=1.0 (need t>3.0 — Harvey-Liu-Zhu)
Per-signal ICmom +0.029 · reversal +0.045 · low-vol −0.041 (suspect)
Does it pay? — cost ladder (gross +4.6%/yr · Sharpe 0.26)
− 5 bps/side+3.2%/yr · Sharpe 0.18
− 10 bps/side+1.8%/yr · Sharpe 0.10
− 20 bps/side−1.1%/yr · Sharpe −0.06
− 30 bps/side−3.9%/yr · Sharpe −0.22
Break-even cost~16 bps/side — below honest all-in (20−30 bps spread+impact + short borrow ~50 bps/yr)
Max drawdown (net)−37.6%
Does it persist?
IC first half (2016−21) → second half (2021−26)+0.042 → −0.00
…though the decay is itself not significant (Δt≈1.0) — you can't separate "real edge that faded" from noise sloshing around zero.
Does breadth rescue it? — the one lever that could
Same model, 85 cherry-picked mega-caps → 589 names (√breadth 9→24)IC +0.021 → −0.003 · gross Sharpe +0.26 → −0.06
Broadening the universe didn't tighten a positive edge — it collapsed it (break-even fell to −3 bps/side: a loss even trading free). The +0.02 was selection in the liquid mega-caps; breadth amplifies real signal, so here it amplified ~nothing and exposed the apparent edge as noise. This is Grinold's law run in reverse: √N × (a true zero) = zero.
No investable edge — and that is the finding. This is the honest, no-fit, free-data version of the only accessible levers (change-the-target + breadth), and it correctly returns ~zero: a full-sample IC indistinguishable from zero, decayed to nothing, with honest costs (turnover + short borrow) eating the entire +4.6% gross. What it proves is epistemic — the accessible levers are honesty instruments that cleanly reject a fake edge, not machines that manufacture a real one. Pros make the same tiny IC pay with what free data can't buy: a delisting-inclusive universe, microcap access (premia ~19% vs ~9%), a securities-lending desk, and execution that cuts turnover below the cost cliff. Demo only — never a signal, never a recommendation.

Forward macro — recession & financial conditions months horizon

Where the best free forward-content data actually carries signal: the yield curve, credit spreads, and financial-conditions index — leading recession and stress at a months horizon (not days). The constructive complement to the vol lab's finding that these overfit a short-horizon model.

Backtest replay & base-rate honesty

Strict walk-forward: at the cursor date, every statistic uses only data up to that day. The panel then looks at what actually happened next — and compares it to the unconditional base rate, because with only a handful of real drawdown events, apparent "skill" is mostly luck.
a synthetic vol-clustering series with no built-in transition

In plain English — what this is telling you right now

Auto-written from the live readings, for a non-specialist. Updates with the data (and with the cursor in Backtest).

Perspectives — documented frameworks as lenses interpretive, not predictive

Live numbers read through ten publicly documented investor/economist frameworks. Each is really a stance — contrarian↔trend, value↔momentum, risk-on↔risk-off, micro↔macro, instability, fat-tails, factor-balance, sizing-discipline — wrapped in a memorable name, chosen so the set spans what this console actually measures. Generated from the current data; interpretive lenses "in the spirit of" each thinker, not their words, endorsements, predictions, or advice. The aggregate view synthesises them.

Structural backdrop — distribution & real economy

The wealth-distribution and real-economy data Gary Stevenson's thesis actually rests on — now on the board (it was the honest gap in his lens). Slow-moving (quarterly/annual, lagged) and US, so it is structural context, not a trading signal.
value = latest / as-of cursorright column = ~10-year change

Fragility thermometer sort: basket ▾

0–100 = how far each asset's recent behavior sits above its own historical norm on three fragility channels (persistence · amplitude · tail). High ≠ crash; it can persist for years. Three mini-bars = the sub-scores.
calmelevatedleast resilient vs own norm mini-bars: persist amplitude tail

Indicator detail —

The six critical-slowing-down-analogue channels behind the score, each with its honest market failure mode. They are not independent votes (persistence channels especially overlap).

Surprise lens (active-inference analogue)

One-step negative log-likelihood of a simple causal AR(1)+EWMA-vol model — an analogue of variational free energy, not the full Active-Inference/EFE engine. Rising = today's moves are more surprising to a simple model.

Shock clustering — Hawkes self-excitation

Do large moves beget more large moves? Fits a self-exciting point process to ≥${'2.5'}σ return shocks; the branching ratio n is how self-reinforcing clustering is (→1 = critical). Realizes your LSAxJEPA Hawkes λi(t); the temporal twin of ρ(Φ)→1.

Scenario sandbox (drill)

Inject a synthetic structure to see how the directed engine responds. Clearly a drill — never live data.

Data health

Embedded real-history snapshot is the floor; live endpoints refresh the tail when reachable. A single feed failure never blanks the console.
